title: High Thermal Conductivity and Biocompatibility Silk Fibroin Hydrogels Filled with CDs@BNOH for Body Surface Cooling
subtitle: ACS Applied Materials & Interfaces 2025

slides.title: High Thermal Conductivity and Biocompatibility Silk Fibroin Hydrogels Filled with CDs@BNOH for Body Surface Cooling
slides.subtitle: ACS Applied Materials & Interfaces 2025
slides.note: ACS abstract, article page, figures 1-5 and conclusion.

cards.title: Why it matters
cards.text: Wearable cooling needs a matrix that is biocompatible, degradable and thermally useful.

cards.title: Main bottleneck
cards.text: Many conductive fillers are good conductors but poor skin-contact materials without surface engineering.

cards.title: Core idea
cards.text: Hydroxylated BN is further modified by carbon quantum dots to improve phonon transport and UV response.

items.title: Wearable passive cooling
items.desc: Hydrogels and textiles begin to merge with cooling goals.

items.title: Biocompatible gels
items.desc: Skin-contact materials become a major design target.

items.title: BNNS/SF cooling films
items.desc: Film-type alignment and radiative cooling are proven.

items.title: CDs@BNOH/SilMA
items.desc: This work adds surface engineering and degradable hydrogel integration.

slides.title: Benchmark 对比
rows: Long-term wear still needs more data

steps.title: Prepare CDs
steps.desc: Make carbon quantum dots from urea/citrate precursors.

steps.title: Build CDs@BNOH
steps.desc: Functionalize hydroxylated BN with CDs to tune the interface.

steps.title: Make SilMA
steps.desc: Methacrylate-modify silk fibroin for UV-curable gelation.

steps.title: Infuse patch
steps.desc: Load the filler into cotton-supported gel and cure into a wearable patch.

slides.title: 核心公式与机理
slides.formula: Proper CDs modification improves phonon transport; too much coating disrupts the BN network.

lines.english: Raman linewidth used as a phonon-lifetime indicator
surface cooling effect relative to blank

cards.title: Method validation
cards.text: Structure, thermal and cooling behavior are all checked.

cards.title: Indoor tests
cards.text: Simulated skin and wearable scenarios are used.

cards.title: Outdoor tests
cards.text: Sunlight or environment are incorporated when relevant.

cards.title: Stability
cards.text: Cycle, wetting or degradation are quantified.

cards.title: Heat transfer
cards.text: The filler network changes phonon pathways and heat spreading.

cards.title: Interface control
cards.text: Surface chemistry decides whether the network remains useful.

cards.title: Skin contact
cards.text: Wearable patches and body surface cooling.

cards.title: Bioelectronics
cards.text: Heat removal from LEDs or soft devices.

cards.title: Reusable settings
cards.text: Washing, cycling or reuse depending on the paper.

slides.title: 局限与展望
limitations: Conductivity is still below the best film-type BNNS systems.
Outdoor performance depends on contact and ambient conditions.
Long-term wear tests are still limited.
futureWork: Quantify comfort under sweat, motion and repeated bending.
Optimize CDs loading to keep the BN phonon network intact.
Explore device integration for wearable electronics.
Test other biodegradable matrices and patch geometries.

items.text: Carbon-dot-modified hydroxylated BN is embedded into a silk hydrogel patch to balance thermal conduction, UV handling, biodegradability and skin-contact cooling.
The main design principle is to align filler geometry, interfacial chemistry and matrix choice with the target cooling mode.
